label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's think through this step by step. So, the question is, if I follow these instructions, do I end up back where I started? The options are Yes or No. First, let me break down the instructions one by one. The starting point is where I am, facing a certain direction. Let's assume I'm facing north initially for simplicity. 1. Turn around. If I turn around, I'll be facing south. So, my direction is now south. 2. Turn left. Turning left from south would make me face east. Because if I'm facing south and turn left (which is 90 degrees to the left), east is to my left when facing south. Wait, let me double-check that. If you face south, your left is east? Hmm. Let's think about the standard directions. If you're facing north, left is west, right is east. If you turn around to face south, your left would be east? Wait, no. If you're facing south, turning left would be east? Wait, no. If I'm facing south, turning left (counter-clockwise) would be east? Wait, no. Let me visualize this. Facing north, left is west. If I turn around (180 degrees), facing south. From south, turning left 90 degrees would face east? Wait, no.
